data.description:
Rename the Stage 11 fork's product brand from c11mux to c11. Scope is fork-level only — upstream manaflow-ai/cmux untouched.

**Heuristic: user sees it → rename; compiler/daemon sees it → don't.**

Rename: product brand in prose, README, CLAUDE.md, PHILOSOPHY.md, CHANGELOG.md, C11MUX_TODO.md → C11_TODO.md, docs/ active plans, skills/ directory names and content, Resources/welcome.md, Resources/Localizable.xcstrings values.

Do NOT rename: CLI binary cmux, env vars (CMUX_*), socket paths, bundle IDs, Xcode targets/schemes, cmuxd daemon, Swift/Zig source identifiers, historical spec/review-pack docs, GhosttyTabs.xcodeproj/.

Execution: 4 parallel agents on disjoint file trees. Briefs at notes/c11-rebrand/. B3 (skills/) runs last to avoid mid-session skill-load conflicts.

Follow-ups: C11-2 (landing + demo video), C11-3 (installer / skill-copy UX), separate ticket for runtime c11 CLI symlink.

Acceptance:
- rg -i c11mux returns only whitelisted historical docs and intentional identifiers
- cmux binary still works; skill still auto-loads under skills/c11/
- Dry-run upstream merge from manaflow-ai/cmux has zero conflicts in Sources/, cmuxd/, Xcode project
- ./scripts/reload.sh --tag c11-rebrand smoke test passes
data.title: c11mux → c11 rebrand pass (fork-level, upstream-compatible)

data.body: Delegator online for completion audit. Worktree at /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11-worktrees/c11-1-completion-audit (off origin/main). Will run all 4 acceptance criteria and report findings before recommending a status transition.

## C11-1 Completion Audit

Audited against `origin/main` at HEAD (commit `3a390811`) on 2026-05-03.

### Criterion 1: `rg -i c11mux`
**Status: PASS WITH MINOR STRAGGLERS** — 784 total hits; ~95% correctly whitelisted (Swift identifiers like `C11muxTheme`, runtime paths like `~/Library/Application Support/c11mux/`, `CMUX_*` env vars, historical review-pack docs in `docs/c11mux-*-review-pack-*/`, historical `notes/`, upstream-compat strings).

Stale items (none user-visible, but worth noting):
- `skills/c11/references/metadata.md:5` — broken link to `docs/c11mux-module-2-metadata-spec.md` (file does not exist).
- `Sources/c11App.swift:3093` — `defaultValue: "c11mux"` in About dialog. Overridden by `Localizable.xcstrings` "c11" translation, so no user-visible effect; cosmetic.
- 4 Source comments reference historical spec docs (`TitleFormatting.swift`, `ContentView.swift`, `AgentChip.swift`, `SurfaceMetadataStore.swift`).
- `Resources/InfoPlist.xcstrings` — keys `"New c11mux Workspace Here"` / `"New c11mux Window Here"` retained but values render as `"New c11 …"` in all 7 locales (no user-visible effect).
- `scripts/generate_c11_*.py` — docstrings still call the brand "c11mux"; `generate_dark_icon.py` and `generate_nightly_icon.py` reference a non-existent `generate_c11mux_icon.py`.
- `docs/c11-charter.md` — three stale plan-stage references including "Homebrew tap name. Probably `stage11/c11mux`".
- `docs/upstream-sync.md` — entire upstream-compat contract doc says to keep `com.stage11.c11mux` bundle IDs and `cmuxApp.swift` filename. Both have been renamed (`com.stage11.c11`, `Sources/c11App.swift`). Doc is stale and contradicts what was actually shipped.

The earlier audit at `notes/c11-audit-2026-04-21.md` §7 flagged a similar drift list as known-pending.

### Criterion 2: `cmux` binary + skill auto-load
**Status: PASS WITH NOTE** — `cmux` at `/Users/atin/.local/bin/cmux` exists (Python shim per `notes/c11-cli-coexistence-plan.md`) but errors with `"no reload-selected dev cmux CLI found. Run ./scripts/reload.sh --tag <name> first."` until a tag is selected. This is by design but is a behavior change from pre-rebrand — flagging in case it surprises downstream readers. `c11 --help` works. `skills/c11/SKILL.md` present with valid frontmatter and the auto-load description fires on `CMUX_SHELL_INTEGRATION=1`. Only c11mux residue in skill content is the one broken link above.

### Criterion 3: dry-run upstream merge for conflicts
**Status: LITERAL FAIL / SPIRITUAL PASS** — 968 upstream commits and 183 c11 commits have landed since the 2026-04-20 rebrand merge. Today's `git merge --no-commit --no-ff upstream/main` produces conflicts in:
- `Sources/`: ~25 files (e.g. `c11App.swift`, `Workspace.swift`, `WorkspaceContentView.swift`, `TabManager.swift`, `TerminalController.swift`, `GhosttyConfig.swift`, theme files, panel files…)
- `GhosttyTabs.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`: ~30+ conflict regions
- `c11Tests/` ~26 files, `c11UITests/` ~9 files
- `daemon/remote/cmd/c11d-remote/` (renamed `cmuxd`): `main.go`, `agent_launch.go`, `tmux_compat.go`, `ws_pty.go`, several test files
- `cmux-Bridging-Header.h`

Sampled conflicts (e.g. `Sources/c11App.swift` `WorkspaceTitlebarSettings`/`TabBarChromeState` block) are **organic feature divergence**, not rebrand-related — the conflicts exist because we shipped features upstream doesn't have. The criterion was meaningful at the time of merge (April 20); 3 weeks + ~1.1k commits later, it is no longer cleanly diagnostic of rebrand impact. Operator call needed on whether to keep this criterion or interpret it spiritually.

### Criterion 4: `./scripts/reload.sh --tag c11-rebrand` smoke test
**Status: PASS (after worktree setup)** — fresh worktree was missing submodules and the prebuilt GhosttyKit. After `git submodule update --init --recursive` and `./scripts/download-prebuilt-ghosttykit.sh`, the build succeeded, the app launched at `/Users/atin/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/c11-c11-rebrand/Build/Products/Debug/c11 DEV c11-rebrand.app`, the socket came up at `/tmp/c11-debug-c11-rebrand.sock`, and the process is running. The initial failures were worktree-env, not a C11-1 regression.

### Recommendation: needs_human

The rebrand is substantively done. User-visible brand surface is c11 everywhere. Remaining `c11mux` hits all fall into the whitelisted categories or are non-user-facing residue.

Two operator calls are needed before closing:
1. **Stragglers (>5, per the audit runbook).** Six minor cosmetic items above. Options:
   a. Absorb into a small fix commit on `c11-1-completion-audit` and complete C11-1.
   b. File a follow-up "rebrand cleanup" ticket and complete C11-1 as-is.
   c. Treat as known-acceptable drift and complete C11-1 as-is.
2. **Criterion 3 interpretation.** Literal text fails today due to organic divergence; spirit passes (rebrand was upstream-compat by design). OK to accept, or want a focused re-check (e.g. dry-run merge against `upstream/main@2026-04-20` to isolate rebrand-only conflicts)?

Auditor recommendation: **(1b) + (2) accept-as-is** — file a small "rebrand stragglers" follow-up and complete C11-1. The user-visible brand work is done; the residue is non-blocking and orthogonal to the rebrand acceptance contract.

Routing to needs_human — see prior findings comment. Two operator calls: (1) what to do with the 6 minor cosmetic stragglers, (2) how to interpret Criterion 3 given 3 weeks + ~1.1k commits of organic divergence. Auditor recommendation: file a small follow-up ticket and close C11-1 as done.

Reconciliation: PR #169 (c11mux → c11 rebrand pass) merged 2026-05-17; rebrand shipped. Operator (atin) confirmed close on 2026-06-03. Residual cleanup branches (c11-1-rebrand-cleanup +11 commits, rename/c11mux-paths-to-c11 +4) remain unmerged; if that work is still wanted it should get a fresh ticket.
